bird.name: Sira tanager
bird.summary: The Sira tanager (Stilpnia phillipsi) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to Peru and exists only in the Sira rang…

relations.subject: Sira tanager
relations.evidence: Between 1969 and 1972, John Terborgh and Weske reported about their collections in the Cerros del Sira on the discovery of this new species of tanager that is related to the black-capped tanager. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests
relations.object_summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

relations.subject: Sira tanager
relations.evidence: It is threatened by habitat loss
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
